tool.name: COCO to LabelMe JSON Converter
tool.description: Convert COCO annotation files into LabelMe-compatible JSON format

param.name: coco_file
param.label: COCO annotation file
param.help: JSON file in COCO format containing images, annotations, and categories.

param.name: path_mode
param.label: Image path mode
param.help: Controls how the 'imagePath' field is written in each output JSON. Choose the mode that matches your local folder structure.

param.name: custom_image_path
param.label: Custom base path
param.help: Base directory prepended to each image filename in 'imagePath' (e.g. '../images' → '../images/photo.jpg').

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**What it does**
Converts a COCO annotation file (JSON) into one LabelMe JSON file per image. Each annotation is transformed into a LabelMe shape (polygon, line, or point) and written with the correct ``imagePath``, dimensions, and metadata.
-----
**Inputs**
- **COCO annotation file**: a standard COCO JSON file with ``images``, ``annotations``, and ``categories`` fields. Multiple files can be provided.
-----
**Image path modes**
+----------------+----------------------------------------------+ | Mode | imagePath written in output | +================+==============================================+ | Simple | ``../filename.jpg`` | +----------------+----------------------------------------------+ | AnyLabeling | ``../input_images/filename.jpg`` | +----------------+----------------------------------------------+ | Custom | ``<your base path>/filename.jpg`` | +----------------+----------------------------------------------+
Choose **Simple** if your images sit one level above the annotation folder.
Choose **AnyLabeling** if you are using the AnyLabeling annotation tool with its default ``input_images/`` directory layout.
Choose **Custom** to specify any arbitrary base path.
-----
**Outputs**
A dataset collection containing one JSON file per image, named after the original image filename (without extension). Each file follows the LabelMe format and can be opened directly in LabelMe or AnyLabeling.
